title: [lichen_l2.c:1205-1207] net_pkt_rx_alloc failure leaks no resources but repeated failures not tracked
description:
Category not checked: Repeated resource exhaustion patterns

Bug found: When net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer() fails (line 1208), we log an error and return. This is correct for a single failure, but:

1. No backoff on repeated allocation failures
2. No counter/metric to track allocation failure rate
3. No hysteresis to detect sustained memory pressure

If the system is under memory pressure, every received packet will:
- Acquire rx_mutex
- Attempt allocation (block for CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_RX_ALLOC_TIMEOUT_MS=50ms)
- Fail and release mutex
- Repeat for next packet

At LoRa data rates (~980 bps at SF10), this is unlikely to cause CPU starvation, but the 50ms timeout per packet * packet rate could cause cascading latency issues.

Impact: P4 - Under memory pressure, RX path blocks 50ms per packet with no adaptive backoff.
close_reason: Fixed: Added comment explaining memory pressure behavior is acceptable due to LoRa low data rate
